title: CDS Study Plan — 6-Week Roadmap
description: A structured 6-week study plan covering all 64 CDS artifacts across 12 phases, with hourly breakdowns, weekly objectives, and advanced topics.

sections.title: Overview & Prerequisites
sections.description:
This study plan organises 64 CDS artifacts across 12 learning phases into a 6-week (~33 hour) curriculum. Each week bundles related phases so concepts build naturally on one another.

Prerequisites

  • Basic ABAP syntax (data types, internal tables, classes)
  • SQL fundamentals (SELECT, JOIN, WHERE, GROUP BY)
  • SAP GUI / ADT (ABAP Development Tools) familiarity

How to use

  • Each week has ~5–6 hours of material — comfortable for one hour per day
  • Open each artifact in the docs panel, read key learning points, then study the CDS source code
  • Practice by modifying the examples in your own SAP S/4HANA or BTP ABAP environment

sections.title: Week 1 — Foundations
sections.description:
Phases 1 & 2 — 6 hours

PhaseTimeArtifacts
1. Fundamentals4hCDS Intro, View Entity Syntax, Simple View, View on View, Data Manipulation, Type Casting
2. Parameters2hInput Parameters, Default, Constant, Variable

Goal: Understand the CDS view syntax, create simple views, work with parameters.

Practice: Write a view that joins transparent tables and accepts a date range parameter.

sections.title: Week 2 — Relationships & Grouping
sections.description:
Phases 3 & 4 — 6 hours

PhaseTimeArtifacts
3. Joins & Associations4hINNER, LEFT, RIGHT, CROSS JOIN, Association, Path Expressions
4. Aggregation & Subqueries2hGROUP BY, HAVING, EXISTS, UNION

Goal: Master join types, understand associations vs JOINs, write aggregate queries.

Practice: Model an association between header and item views; write a join query with aggregation.

sections.title: Week 3 — Functions, Client & Session
sections.description:
Phases 5 & 6 + partial 9 — 5 hours

PhaseTimeArtifacts
5. Built-in Functions2hString, Date, COALESCE, Currency Conversion
6. VDM2hPrivate, Basic, Composite, Consumption Views
9. Advanced (partial)1hClient Handling, Session Variables

Goal: Use built-in functions, apply VDM layering, handle client and session context.

Practice: Build a 4-layer VDM stack (Private → Basic → Composite → Consumption) with currency conversion.

sections.title: Week 4 — Fiori, OData & RAP
sections.description:
Phases 7 & 8 — 6 hours

PhaseTimeArtifacts
7. Fiori & OData3hMetadata Extension, UI Annotations, Value Help, Search, OData Draft, Service Expose
8. RAP & Extensibility3hComposition, Object Model, RAP Behavior, Extensibility, View Extension, Numbering

Goal: Annotate views for Fiori, expose OData services, define RAP behavior with early/late numbering.

Practice: Create a full RAP stack: CDS → Behavior → Service → UI annotations → Draft-enabled Fiori app.

sections.title: Week 5 — Advanced Modeling & Analytics
sections.description:
Phases 9 & 10 — 6 hours

PhaseTimeArtifacts
9. Advanced Data Modeling4hHierarchy, Text Association, Virtual Elements, Custom Entities, Abstract Entities, Table Entities, Projection Views, JSON/XML
10. Analytics & Security2hDCL & Access Control, Analytical CDS, Analytical V3, Release Contracts

Goal: Model complex CDS patterns, secure data with DCL, build analytical star schemas.

Practice: Design an analytical CDS view with cube/query; secure it with instance-based DCL.

sections.title: Week 6 — Enterprise Features
sections.description:
Phases 11 & 12 — 4 hours

PhaseTimeArtifacts
11. Table Functions & AMDP2hSimple TF, Parameterized TF, Currency TF, Advanced TF, Simple AMDP, Dynamic AMDP
12. Quality & Enterprise2hPerformance, Unit Testing, RAP Eventing, Cloud Core

Goal: Implement table functions, write AMDPs, optimise performance, test and deploy cloud-ready CDS.

Practice: Create an AMDP-based table function, write ABAP unit tests with CDS test doubles, apply performance rules.

sections.title: Beyond the Plan
sections.description:
These advanced topics extend beyond the 64 core artifacts. Refer to SAP Help Portal and SAP Community for deep dives.

TopicDescription
CDS-based BW Bridge / ODPExtract CDS data into SAP BW using ODP context and extraction views
Fiori Elements Advanced UXCustom sections, multi-edit tables, guided procedures, and flexible column layouts
Multi-tenant DCLField-level vs instance-level authorization in cloud and on-premise multi-tenant systems
Advanced RAP DeterminationsSide-effects, dynamic actions, feature control, and internal vs external numbering
CDS with External OData/RFCConsuming CDS views via RAP outbound service or OData client proxy
CDS Entity Manipulation Language (EML)Programmatic `MODIFY ENTITIES` / `READ ENTITIES` for transactional operations
CDS-based Travel / RAP GeneratorSAP Fiori reference scenario and RAP generator for rapid bootstrapping
CDS View Maintenance (SMC)Using `@Metadata.allowExtensions` for custom fields and app extensibility

content:
Overview & Prerequisites

This study plan organises 64 CDS artifacts across 12 learning phases into a 6-week (~33 hour) curriculum. Each week bundles related phases so concepts build naturally on one another.

Prerequisites

  • Basic ABAP syntax (data types, internal tables, classes)
  • SQL fundamentals (SELECT, JOIN, WHERE, GROUP BY)
  • SAP GUI / ADT (ABAP Development Tools) familiarity

How to use

  • Each week has ~5–6 hours of material — comfortable for one hour per day
  • Open each artifact in the docs panel, read key learning points, then study the CDS source code
  • Practice by modifying the examples in your own SAP S/4HANA or BTP ABAP environment

Week 1 — Foundations

Phases 1 & 2 — 6 hours

PhaseTimeArtifacts
1. Fundamentals4hCDS Intro, View Entity Syntax, Simple View, View on View, Data Manipulation, Type Casting
2. Parameters2hInput Parameters, Default, Constant, Variable

Goal: Understand the CDS view syntax, create simple views, work with parameters.

Week 2 — Relationships & Grouping

Phases 3 & 4 — 6 hours

PhaseTimeArtifacts
3. Joins & Associations4hINNER, LEFT, RIGHT, CROSS JOIN, Association, Path Expressions
4. Aggregation & Subqueries2hGROUP BY, HAVING, EXISTS, UNION

Week 3 — Functions, Client & Session

Phases 5 & 6 + partial 9 — 5 hours

PhaseTimeArtifacts
5. Built-in Functions2hString, Date, COALESCE, Currency Conversion
6. VDM2hPrivate, Basic, Composite, Consumption Views
9. Advanced (partial)1hClient Handling, Session Variables

Week 4 — Fiori, OData & RAP

Phases 7 & 8 — 6 hours

PhaseTimeArtifacts
7. Fiori & OData3hMetadata Extension, UI Annotations, Value Help, Search, OData Draft, Service Expose
8. RAP & Extensibility3hComposition, Object Model, RAP Behavior, Extensibility, View Extension, Numbering

Week 5 — Advanced Modeling & Analytics

Phases 9 & 10 — 6 hours

PhaseTimeArtifacts
9. Advanced Data Modeling4hHierarchy, Text Association, Virtual Elements, Custom Entities, Abstract Entities, Table Entities, Projection Views, JSON/XML
10. Analytics & Security2hDCL & Access Control, Analytical CDS, Analytical V3, Release Contracts

Week 6 — Enterprise Features

Phases 11 & 12 — 4 hours

PhaseTimeArtifacts
11. Table Functions & AMDP2hSimple TF, Parameterized TF, Currency TF, Advanced TF, Simple AMDP, Dynamic AMDP
12. Quality & Enterprise2hPerformance, Unit Testing, RAP Eventing, Cloud Core

Beyond the Plan

These advanced topics extend beyond the 64 core artifacts. Refer to SAP Help Portal and SAP Community for deep dives.

TopicDescription
CDS-based BW Bridge / ODPExtract CDS data into SAP BW using ODP context and extraction views
Fiori Elements Advanced UXCustom sections, multi-edit tables, guided procedures, and flexible column layouts
Multi-tenant DCLField-level vs instance-level authorization in cloud and on-premise multi-tenant systems
Advanced RAP DeterminationsSide-effects, dynamic actions, feature control, and internal vs external numbering
CDS with External OData/RFCConsuming CDS views via RAP outbound service or OData client proxy
CDS Entity Manipulation Language (EML)Programmatic `MODIFY ENTITIES` / `READ ENTITIES` for transactional operations
CDS-based Travel / RAP GeneratorSAP Fiori reference scenario and RAP generator for rapid bootstrapping
CDS View Maintenance (SMC)Using `@Metadata.allowExtensions` for custom fields and app extensibility
topic: 6-week structured curriculum for learning ABAP CDS
